lines.gurmukhi: ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਣੇ ਸਿ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਕਹੀਅਹਿ ਨਾ ਉਰਵਾਰਿ ਨ ਪਾਰੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: bharam bhulaane si manamukh kaheeeh naa uravaar na paare |3|
lines.english: Those who wander around, deluded by doubt, are called manmukhs; they are neither on this side, nor on the other side. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਸੋਈ ਜਨੁ ਪਾਏ ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਸਮੑਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: jis no nadar kare soee jan paae gur kaa sabad samaale |
lines.english: That humble being, who is blessed by the Lord's Glance of Grace obtains Him, and contemplates the Word of the Guru's Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਭਾਗੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਜਿਸੁ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਕਾਲਹਿ ਮਾਰਿ ਬਿਦਾਰੇ ॥੪॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak bhaag hovai jis masatak kaaleh maar bidaare |4|1|
lines.english: O Nanak, one who has such destiny inscribed upon his forehead, conquers and destroys death. ||4||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਮਾਇਆ ਮਾਹਿ ਨਿਸਤਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: har jan maaeaa maeh nisataare |
lines.english: In the midst of Maya, the Lord's servant is emancipated.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਲਾਵਲੁ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: bilaaval mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Bilaaval, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਤੁਲੁ ਕਿਉ ਤੋਲਿਆ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: atul kiau toliaa jaae |
lines.english: How can the unweighable be weighed?

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਜਾ ਹੋਇ ਤ ਸੋਝੀ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: doojaa hoe ta sojhee paae |
lines.english: If there is anyone else as great, then he alone could understand the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ਵਸੈ ਮਨਿ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaad vasai man aae |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, He comes to dwell in the mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਦੀ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਕਿਕੂ ਹੋਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: tis dee keemat kikoo hoe |1|
lines.english: How can His value be estimated? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਤੇ ਦੂਜਾ ਨਾਹੀ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: tis te doojaa naahee koe |
lines.english: There is no other than Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਪਰਖੇ ਆਪਿ ਚਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: aape parakhe aap chalaae |
lines.english: He Himself analyzes the coin, and He Himself approves it as currency.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕੋ ਜਾਣੈ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: taa ko jaanai dubidhaa jaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: One comes to know Him, when duality departs. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਸਰਾਫੁ ਕਸਵਟੀ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: aap saraaf kasavattee laae |
lines.english: He Himself is the Assayer, applying the touch-stone to test it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਤੋਲੇ ਪੂਰਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: aape tole pooraa hoe |
lines.english: He Himself weights it perfectly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਕਾ ਰੂਪੁ ਸਭੁ ਤਿਸ ਤੇ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: maaeaa kaa roop sabh tis te hoe |
lines.english: All the forms of Maya emanate from Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਮੇਲੇ ਸੁ ਨਿਰਮਲੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: jis no mele su niramal hoe |
lines.english: He alone becomes pure and immaculate, who is united with the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਜਾਣੈ ਏਕੋ ਸੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aape jaanai eko soe |2|
lines.english: He alone knows; He is the One and Only Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਲਿਵ ਧਾਤੁ ਹੈ ਆਪੇ ॥
lines.translit: aape liv dhaat hai aape |
lines.english: He Himself leads the mortals to focus on Him, and He Himself causes them to chase after Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਸਚੁ ਦਿਖਾਲੇ ਤਾ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: sabh sach dikhaale taa sach samaae |3|
lines.english: All Truth is revealed to him, and then, he merges in the True Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਲਾਏ ਲਗੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: jis no laae lagai tis aae |
lines.english: He alone is attached, whom the Lord attaches.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸਬਦੁ ਹੈ ਆਪੇ ॥
lines.translit: aape satigur sabad hai aape |
lines.english: He Himself is the True Guru, and He Himself is the Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਆਖਿ ਸੁਣਾਏ ਆਪੇ ॥੪॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak aakh sunaae aape |4|2|
lines.english: O Nanak, He Himself speaks and teaches. ||4||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਬੁਝਾਏ ਆਪੇ ਜਾਪੇ ॥
lines.translit: aap bujhaae aape jaape |
lines.english: He Himself imparts understanding, and He reveals Himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਲਾਵਲੁ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: bilaaval mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Bilaaval, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬ ਤੇ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਸੇਵ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਤੇ ਕਿਆ ਕੋ ਕਹੈ ਬਹਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: saahib te sevak sev saahib te kiaa ko kahai bahaanaa |
lines.english: My Lord and Master has made me His servant, and blessed me with His service; how can anyone argue about this?

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਸਾ ਇਕੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਖੇਲੁ ਬਨਿਆ ਹੈ ਸਭ ਮਹਿ ਏਕੁ ਸਮਾਨਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aisaa ik teraa khel baniaa hai sabh meh ek samaanaa |1|
lines.english: Such is Your play, One and Only Lord; You are the One, contained among all. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਕਰਮੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਸੋ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪਾਏ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਲਾਗੈ ਸਹਜ ਧਿਆਨਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jis karam hovai so satigur paae anadin laagai sehaj dhiaanaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: One who is blessed by the Lord's Mercy, finds the True Guru; night and day, he automatically remains focused on the Lord's meditation. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਪਰਚੈ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਸਮਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: satigur parachai har naam samaanaa |
lines.english: When the True Guru is pleased and appeased, one is absorbed in the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਕੋਈ ਤੇਰੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰੇ ਕਿਆ ਕੋ ਕਰੇ ਅਭਿਮਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa koee teree sevaa kare kiaa ko kare abhimaanaa |
lines.english: How can I serve You? How can I be proud of this?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਅਪੁਨੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਖਿੰਚਹਿ ਤੂ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਤਬ ਕੋਈ ਕਰਉ ਦਿਖਾ ਵਖਿਆਨਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jab apunee jot khincheh too suaamee tab koee krau dikhaa vakhiaanaa |2|
lines.english: When You withdraw Your Light, O Lord and Master, then who can speak and teach? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਗੁਰੁ ਚੇਲਾ ਹੈ ਆਪੇ ਆਪੇ ਗੁਣੀ ਨਿਧਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: aape gur chelaa hai aape aape gunee nidhaanaa |
lines.english: You Yourself are the Guru, and You Yourself are the chaylaa, the humble disciple; You Yourself are the treasure of virtue.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਤੂ ਸਾਚਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਕਉਣੁ ਜਾਣੈ ਤੇਰੇ ਕਾਮਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: kehat naanak too saachaa saahib kaun jaanai tere kaamaan |
lines.english: Says Nanak, You are the True Lord and Master; who can know Your actions?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਆਪਿ ਚਲਾਏ ਤਿਵੈ ਕੋਈ ਚਾਲੈ ਜਿਉ ਹਰਿ ਭਾਵੈ ਭਗਵਾਨਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jiau aap chalaae tivai koee chaalai jiau har bhaavai bhagavaanaa |3|
lines.english: As You cause us to move, so do we move, according to the Pleasure of Your Will, O Lord God. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨਾ ਘਰ ਮਹਿ ਦੇ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਇਕਿ ਭਰਮਿ ਭਵਹਿ ਅਭਿਮਾਨਾ ॥੪॥੩॥
lines.translit: eikanaa ghar meh de vaddiaaee ik bharam bhaveh abhimaanaa |4|3|
lines.english: Some are blessed with glory in their own homes, while others wander in doubt and pride. ||4||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਲਾਵਲੁ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: bilaaval mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Bilaaval, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਾ ਥਾਟੁ ਬਣਾਇਆ ਪੂਰੈ ਵੇਖਹੁ ਏਕ ਸਮਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: pooraa thaatt banaaeaa poorai vekhahu ek samaanaa |
lines.english: The perfect Lord has fashioned the Perfect Creation. Behold the Lord pervading everywhere.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਪਰਪੰਚ ਮਹਿ ਸਾਚੇ ਨਾਮ ਕੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਮਤੁ ਕੋ ਧਰਹੁ ਗੁਮਾਨਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: eis parapanch meh saache naam kee vaddiaaee mat ko dharahu gumaanaa |1|
lines.english: In this play of the world, is the glorious greatness of the True Name. No one should take pride in himself. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਮਤਿ ਆਵੈ ਸੋ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮਾਹਿ ਸਮਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kee jis no mat aavai so satigur maeh samaanaa |
lines.english: One who accepts the wisdom of the True Guru's Teachings, is absorbed into the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਹੁ ਜੁਗਾ ਕਾ ਹੁਣਿ ਨਿਬੇੜਾ ਨਰ ਮਨੁਖਾ ਨੋ ਏਕੁ ਨਿਧਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: chahu jugaa kaa hun niberraa nar manukhaa no ek nidhaanaa |
lines.english: Now, this is the essence of the teachings of the four ages: for the human race, the Name of the One Lord is the greatest treasure.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹ ਬਾਣੀ ਜੋ ਜੀਅਹੁ ਜਾਣੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਰਵੈ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: eih baanee jo jeeahu jaanai tis antar ravai har naamaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The Lord's Name abides deep within the nucleus of one who realizes the Bani of the Guru's Word within his soul. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਤੁ ਸੰਜਮ ਤੀਰਥ ਓਨਾ ਜੁਗਾ ਕਾ ਧਰਮੁ ਹੈ ਕਲਿ ਮਹਿ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jat sanjam teerath onaa jugaa kaa dharam hai kal meh keerat har naamaa |2|
lines.english: Celibacy, self-discipline and pilgrimages were the essence of Dharma in those past ages; but in this Dark Age of Kali Yuga, the Praise of the Lord's Name is the essence of Dharma. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੁਗਿ ਜੁਗਿ ਆਪੋ ਆਪਣਾ ਧਰਮੁ ਹੈ ਸੋਧਿ ਦੇਖਹੁ ਬੇਦ ਪੁਰਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: jug jug aapo aapanaa dharam hai sodh dekhahu bed puraanaa |
lines.english: Each and every age has its own essence of Dharma; study the Vedas and the Puraanas, and see this as true.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਿਨੀ ਧਿਆਇਆ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਜਗਿ ਤੇ ਪੂਰੇ ਪਰਵਾਨਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: guramukh jinee dhiaaeaa har har jag te poore paravaanaa |3|
lines.english: They are Gurmukh, who meditate on the Lord, Har, Har; in this world, they are perfect and approved. ||3||
